name: Shanghai Style Sweet and Sour Ribs
recipeIngredient: 3 lb Pork spareribs, cut across bones into 1 1/2 inch length
recipeInstructions:
This recipe is not for the barbecue, but I thought I'd share it anyway.
Don't let the simplicity of this recipe fool you. It's one of the best sweet and sour rib recipes I've come across and also one of the easiest to prepare. The flavor of the ribs will stand out rather than being overshadowed by more complex sweet and sour sauces.
1. In a 2-quart stainless steel saucepan, combine sugar and water. Bring mixture to a boil over moderate heat, stirring until the sugar dissolves.
2. Increase heat to high and cook, uncovered, for 5 minutes or until the syrup reaches 200°F on a candy thermometer.
3. Reduce heat to low, stir in soy sauce, molasses, ground galangal, ground cilantro, and black pepper. Simmer for 3 minutes.
4. Remove from heat and let cool.
5. Strain the sauce through a fine sieve set over a bowl. The sauce will keep at room temperature for 2 to 3 months if tightly covered.
